analysis.core_story_summary: This austerity is related with the worship of lord Kartikeya and is observed in the bright half of the hindu month-pausha. On the sixth day of this month,a devotee is expected to live on nothing else but fruits. Lord Kartikeya is also known by several other names-Shadanan,Senani,Kritikasut,Kumar,Skand,etc.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: Mostly suitable for broad audiences with light cultural context.

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **Kaam-Vrata — Kids (8-12):** This austerity is related with the worship of lord Kartikeya and is observed in the bright half of the hindu month-pausha. On the sixth day of this month,a devotee is expected to live on nothing else but fruits. Lord Kartikeya is also known by several other names-Shadanan,Senani,Kritikasut,Kumar,Skand,etc. This story teaches actions have consequences, humility before higher wisdom. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.teens_13_17: **Kaam-Vrata — Teens (13-17):** This austerity is related with the worship of lord Kartikeya and is observed in the bright half of the hindu month-pausha. On the sixth day of this month,a devotee is expected to live on nothing else but fruits. Lord Kartikeya is also known by several other names-Shadanan,Senani,Kritikasut,Kumar,Skand,etc. For teens, the key idea is actions have consequences, humility before higher wisdom. It shows how choices, discipline, and character shape outcomes.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.young_adults_18_30: **Kaam-Vrata — Young Adults:** This austerity is related with the worship of lord Kartikeya and is observed in the bright half of the hindu month-pausha. On the sixth day of this month,a devotee is expected to live on nothing else but fruits. Lord Kartikeya is also known by several other names-Shadanan,Senani,Kritikasut,Kumar,Skand,etc. A modern takeaway is actions have consequences, humility before higher wisdom. The narrative can be read as a reflection on purpose, responsibility, and values-driven action.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.devotional_traditional: **Kaam-Vrata — Devotional/Traditional Readers:** This austerity is related with the worship of lord Kartikeya and is observed in the bright half of the hindu month-pausha. On the sixth day of this month,a devotee is expected to live on nothing else but fruits. Lord Kartikeya is also known by several other names-Shadanan,Senani,Kritikasut,Kumar,Skand,etc. The episode highlights actions have consequences, humility before higher wisdom. It preserves reverence for divine agency while encouraging faithful practice and humility.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.global_general: **Kaam-Vrata — Global General Audience:** This austerity is related with the worship of lord Kartikeya and is observed in the bright half of the hindu month-pausha. On the sixth day of this month,a devotee is expected to live on nothing else but fruits. Lord Kartikeya is also known by several other names-Shadanan,Senani,Kritikasut,Kumar,Skand,etc. In universal terms, it explores actions have consequences, humility before higher wisdom. Cultural names are preserved while the message is presented in accessible contemporary English.
